title: CF Movement Trigger Contracts
source_policy: Derived from the decoded CF sector-list rows, the CF door-data payload contract, and the local C0 dispatcher/helper bodies. This names the trigger payload word per movement trigger type only where C0 consumer behavior supports it.

movement_trigger_entry.consumer: C0:7477 compares this byte against the low 5 bits of the input X-side coordinate
C0:7477 compares this byte against the low 5 bits of the input Y-side coordinate
C0:7526 dispatches this byte to type-specific movement/transition helpers
C0:7477 stores this word in $5DBC; the dispatcher passes it to the selected helper

trigger_payload_word_by_type.consumer: C0:6A1B masks the word to a CF DOOR_DATA offset and reads a type 0 event-gated script pointer record
C0:6A91 writes movement state 0x0007 when zero and 0x0008 when nonzero
C0:6ACA masks the word to a CF DOOR_DATA offset and queues it as a type 2 door-transition pointer
C0:6E6E uses bit 15 for the resume branch and otherwise uses the high byte as a staged offset-step selector
C0:70CB uses the high byte as a 0..3 staged movement selector for C3:E200/E208/E210/E218/E220/E228 tables
C0:7526 passes the word to C0:6A8B, whose local body is REP #$31; RTS
C0:65C2 uses type 6 lookups to resolve a CF DOOR_DATA cached interaction pointer into $5DDE/$5DE0

trigger_type_summaries.source_emission_note: join payload word to a type 0 DOOR_DATA event-gated script pointer record
emit payload as numeric state selector; zero selects 0x0007, nonzero selects 0x0008
join payload word to a type 2 DOOR_DATA transition record
emit payload as numeric offset-step mode word; bit 15 is the resume branch
emit payload as numeric staged-movement mode word; high byte is selector 0..3
emit payload as no-op numeric word; many values are pointer-like but the selected C0 body is no-op
join payload word to a type 6 DOOR_DATA cached-interaction pointer record
